results.title: Sampaguita vendor killed by drinking buddies in Antipolo
results.description: A 42-year-old sampaguita vendor was killed after being struck with a glass bottle and stabbed following an argument during a drinking session in Antipolo City, Rizal, on July 5. The Antipolo Component City Police Station (Antipolo CCPS) said the incident occurred around 11:25 p.m. at Sitio Gumamela 2, Phase 5, Barangay Sta. Cruz, where the victim was drinking with two suspects whose identities were not disclosed. According to witnesses, an altercation suddenly ensued between the victim and the suspects while they were drinking. Police investigation revealed that one of the suspects struck the victim on the head with a glass bottle, causing him to fall to the ground. The other suspect allegedly stabbed the victim repeatedly with a bladed weapon in different parts of the body. Both suspects immediately fled. The victim was rushed to the hospital but was declared dead on arrival. On Tuesday, July 7, the Rizal Police Provincial Office (Rizal PPO) said the suspects have been arrested and may face homicide charges.

results.title: Assam Assembly Raises Concerns Over Jal Jeevan Mission Implementation
results.description: The Assam Legislative Assembly recently scrutinized the Jal Jeevan Mission, highlighting significant issues such as non-functional water supply schemes and arsenic contamination in drinking water. MLAs raised concerns about discrepancies in official records and the reliability of water access. Public Health Engineering Minister Krishnendu Paul assured that the government is committed to addressing these shortcomings. The discussion revealed alarming claims about operational schemes failing to deliver water, prompting calls for immediate action. The assembly's focus on these pressing issues reflects ongoing controversies surrounding the implementation of the Centre's flagship drinking water initiative, raising questions about fund management and accountability.

results.title: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announces IIM Bangalore campus in Indonesia
results.description: IIM Bangalore will establish its first overseas campus in Indonesia. This initiative aims to benefit youth across the entire ASEAN region. The campus will offer executive and degree-granting programs. It will focus on areas like artificial intelligence and digital transformation. This collaboration strengthens India-Indonesia educational ties significantly.
